title: Inference Recon — Findings Envelope
description: Top-level report produced by an AI-driven security review. v0.2 adds data_profile; v0.3 adds threat_model with Level 1 DFD and dfd_element on findings.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema. Bumped to 0.3 to add the required threat_model / DFD object.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the scan was produced.

model.description: Identifier of the model that produced the findings (e.g., 'claude-opus-4-7').

prompt_version.description: Version of the orchestration prompt used. Tracked separately from schema_version so prompt iteration can be measured against a stable schema.

duration_seconds.description: Optional. Wall-clock time of the scan.

size_budget_status.description: Whether the project fits within the published size budget for this version.

commit_hash.description: Optional. Git commit hash of the scanned state.

stack_packs_loaded.description: Names of stack-specific rule packs loaded for this scan. Empty if none.

data_profile.description: Assessment of what data types this application handles and which regulatory frameworks likely apply. Always emitted. The model reasons from model/schema definitions, migration files, validation schemas, third-party SDK imports, env var names, and documentation in the repo. This profile is the risk multiplier for interpreting findings — the same finding has materially different implications at different sensitivity tiers.

categories.description: Data categories detected in the codebase with supporting evidence.

regulatory_flags.description: Regulatory frameworks assessed for applicability based on detected data categories.

context_note.description: Optional. 1–3 sentence narrative contextualising the sensitivity tier and its implications for interpreting this report's findings.

threat_model.description: Threat model artifacts derived from the codebase. v0.3 contains the Level 1 DFD only. Future versions may add STRIDE tables, attack surface enumeration, and finding-to-node cross-references.

dfd.description: Level 1 Data Flow Diagram. Actors, processes, data stores, data flows, and trust boundaries derived from source code inventory.

mermaid.description: Mermaid flowchart source. Rendered in the HTML report via mermaid.js; emitted in the markdown report as a ```mermaid fenced block. Node IDs should be stable lowercase identifiers (e.g. users_db, app_server) to support future finding cross-referencing via dfd_element.

notes.description: Optional. Caveats about what was inferred vs. directly observed, significant simplifications made, or elements that could not be determined from source (e.g. 'deployment topology inferred from docker-compose.yml; actual network segmentation unknown').

scorecard.description: Letter grade per scope domain. DERIVED from findings; not authored independently by the model.

notes.description: Scan limitations, files skipped, caveats, or other meta information the reader should know.

sensitivity_tier.description: minimal: basic identity/session data only, no regulatory triggers. standard: contact info, basic profile data; GDPR/CCPA may apply. elevated: government IDs, precise financial metadata, location history, or combinations that together raise stakes. high: payment card data (PCI-DSS), PHI (HIPAA), biometrics (BIPA), or GDPR special categories. critical: multiple high-sensitivity categories simultaneously, or children's data (COPPA).

data_category.description: identity_basic: name, email, username, profile picture. contact_info: phone, postal address, IP-as-identifier. auth_credentials: passwords (hashed or plain), session tokens, MFA seeds, OAuth tokens. financial_payment: card numbers, CVV, PANs, expiry dates — PCI-DSS scope. financial_account: bank account/routing numbers, ACH data — GLBA scope. government_id: SSN, TIN, passport, driver's licence, national ID. health_phi: diagnoses, medications, insurance IDs, medical record numbers — HIPAA scope. biometric: fingerprints, facial recognition data, voice prints. location: GPS coordinates, movement history, precise location tracking. childrens_data: any data about users under 13 — COPPA scope. gdpr_special_category: racial/ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, sexual orientation. other_sensitive: anything sensitive not covered above; requires explanation in evidence_summary.

evidence_summary.description: Concise description of where this data type was found: field names, file paths, SDK names, env var names. Not a verbatim quote — a human-readable pointer (e.g., 'card_number, cvv columns in app/models/payment.py; stripe SDK in requirements.txt').

locations.description: Optional specific file locations when evidence is quotable.

applicability.description: likely: detected data almost certainly triggers this regulation. possibly: could apply depending on volume, geography, or context not visible in code. unlikely: not triggered by what was found, but included to show the assessment was made.

rationale.description: One sentence explaining why this regulation was assessed at this applicability level.

category.description: Closed enum. Use the most specific applicable category. Domain buckets (CODE_*, DEP_*, SECRET_*, CONFIG_*, ARCH_*) are preferred when applicable; the named non-prefixed categories (AUTHN_failure, AUTHZ_failure, INSECURE_DESIGN, INTEGRITY_failure) cover OWASP categories without a clean domain equivalent.

quote.description: Verbatim excerpt from the source. Used to ground the finding and let the reader audit the model's interpretation.

cvss_vector.description: Optional. Only populated for findings tied to a published CVE — the model copies the authoritative upstream score; it does NOT author CVSS values itself.

id.description: Stable identifier. Derivation: first 8 hex chars of sha1(category + '|' + evidence.file + '|' + str(evidence.line_start)). Title is excluded so the same finding gets the same ID across runs regardless of how the model phrased the title.

owasp_mapping.description: Optional cross-reference to OWASP Top 10 categories.

title.description: One-line summary. Should be specific and quotable; generic titles like 'Input validation issue' are not acceptable.

related_locations.description: Additional locations relevant to the finding.

exploitation_path.description: Concrete description of how this finding becomes a real-world exploit or harm. Required when severity is critical or high, OR when category begins with ARCH_.

remediation.description: Concrete, actionable fix. Avoids generic advice like 'sanitize inputs'.

references.description: External references: CWE IDs, vendor documentation, CVE IDs, OWASP cheat sheet URLs, etc.

dfd_element.description: Optional. Reference to a Mermaid node ID in threat_model.dfd.mermaid (e.g. 'admin_panel', 'users_db'). Reserved for future finding-to-diagram cross-referencing and HTML interactivity. Set it now; use it later.

allOf.description: exploitation_path required when severity is critical or high.
exploitation_path required for all ARCH_* findings regardless of severity.
cvss field is only meaningful when the finding cites a CVE in references.
